source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Enable audit logging for IBM Cloud Identity and Access Management

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Enable audit logging for IBM Cloud Identity and Access Management
input_row.description: Use the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service to monitor certain IAM events.
input_row.rationale: As a security officer, auditor, or manager, you can use the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service to track how users and applications interact with IBM Cloud Identity and Access Management (IAM). You can use this service to investigate abnormal activity and critical actions and comply with regulatory audit requirements. Impact: Activity Tracker with LogDNA allows you to track the following IAM events: • Managing access groups by creating and deleting groups or adding and removing users • Creating, updating, or deleting service IDs • Creating, updating, or deleting API keys • Creating, updating, or deleting access policies • Logging in to IBM Cloud by using an API key, authorization code, passcode, password, or an API key that is associated with a service ID
input_row.audit: Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Go to the Menu icon. Then, select Observability to access the Observability dashboard. 3. Select Activity Tracker from the page navigation menu 4. Check that you can see an IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA instance in Frankfurt and one instance for each location where you operate in the IBM Cloud. API: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/Activity-Tracker-with-LogDNA?topic=Activity-Tracker-with- LogDNA-at_events_iam#at_events_iam_apikeys
input_row.remediation: You must create an instance of the IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA service in the Frankfurt region to start tracking IAM events. Use a minimum of a 7-day event search. Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. Go to the Menu icon. Then, select Observability to access the Observability dashboard. 3. Select Activity Tracker from the page navigation menu 4. Click Create instance to create an instance of IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA . 5. In the Select a region drop down, choose Frankfurt 6. Select a pricing plan, service name, resource group, and provide optional tags. Choose a plan that offers a minimum of 7-day event search. 7. Click Create . Default Value: By default, audit logging with Activity Tracker with LogDNA is not enabled. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/account
input_row.impact: Activity Tracker with LogDNA allows you to track the following IAM events: • Managing access groups by creating and deleting groups or adding and removing users • Creating, updating, or deleting service IDs • Creating, updating, or deleting API keys • Creating, updating, or deleting access policies • Logging in to IBM Cloud by using an API key, authorization code, passcode, password, or an API key that is associated with a service ID

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- The IBM Cloud Activity Tracker with LogDNA is a standard IBM Cloud service that supports logging and monitoring IAM events.
- The audit instructions specify the use of the IBM Cloud Console and provide specific API documentation, indicating that these checks can be automated.
- The IBM Cloud CLI and APIs allow for querying and managing service instances and configurations.
- The compliance details focus on a technical configuration that can be queried using IBM Cloud APIs or CLI.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_activity_tracker_logdna_iam_logging_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- Use the IBM Cloud CLI or the IBM Cloud API to query the existence of an IBM Cloud Activity Tracker instance for IAM event logging.
- CLI Command Example: `ibmcloud resource service-instances --service-name activity-tracker`
- Validate that an Activity Tracker instance exists in the Frankfurt region.
- Ensure the instance's configuration includes the capture of IAM events, applying the criteria mentioned in the control's description and remediation instructions.
- Expected Configuration: An instance of Activity Tracker with LogDNA in the Frankfurt region should be active with a logging configuration that includes IAM events.
- Pass Criteria: The presence of a correctly configured Activity Tracker instance that logs IAM events in the specified region.
- Fail Criteria: Absence of such an instance, or misconfigured logging that does not include the required IAM events.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
